content_en:
The treatment of language theory in the Kashf al-Asrar is one of the most important parts of the work. Al-Bazdawi's original text, and al-Bukhari's expansion of it, articulates the Hanafi theory of linguistic expression in considerable detail — a theory that differs from the Shafi'i approach in ways that have significant legal consequences. The Hanafi classification of expressions (khass, 'amm, mushatarak, majaz, sarih, kinayah, and others) and the rules governing their interpretation provide the linguistic foundation for Hanafi legal reasoning.

The discussion of commands and their default implications is particularly important. The Hanafi position on whether commands in divine texts default to obligation (wujub) or something less stringent differs subtly from Shafi'i positions and has consequences for how specific Qur'anic and hadith directives are interpreted. al-Bukhari's commentary unpacks these differences with care and defends the Hanafi position against Shafi'i critiques.

The treatment of the Sunnah reflects the Hanafi school's distinctive approach. Al-Bukhari explains and defends the Hanafi position on the legal weight of ahad (single-chain) hadith, the conditions under which weak hadith can be cited as legal evidence, and the relationship between Sunnah and Quran in specification and restriction. The Hanafi approach is more permissive in citing weak hadith and more restrictive in allowing the Sunnah to restrict Qur'anic generalities — positions that require careful justification against Shafi'i objections.

The sections on istihsan receive extensive treatment, as might be expected given that this methodology is the most controversial distinctively Hanafi contribution to usul al-fiqh. Al-Bukhari presents a systematic defense of istihsan, developing al-Bazdawi's account of its foundations and responding to Al-Shafi'i's famous condemnation of it as equivalent to arbitrariness. The defense distinguishes istihsan from arbitrary preference and shows how its criteria constrain its application.

The treatment of ijma' (consensus) includes the Hanafi school's distinctive positions on what types of consensus are binding and how consensus is established. The Hanafi school's particular openness to the consensus of the people of Medina (as represented in Malik's approach) compared to the Shafi'i restriction of binding consensus to universal scholarly agreement is discussed and explained.

A recurring theme throughout the work is the relationship between the transmitted positions of the early Hanafi masters and the theoretical principles that explain them. al-Bukhari's project is simultaneously to articulate a rigorous theory and to show that this theory correctly explains the rulings of the school's founding generation.
content_id:
Pembahasan teori bahasa dalam Kasyf al-Asrar merupakan salah satu bagian terpenting dari karya ini. Teks asli al-Bazdawi, dan pengembangannya oleh al-Bukhari, mengartikulasikan teori Hanafi tentang ungkapan linguistik secara terperinci, sebuah teori yang berbeda dari pendekatan Syafi'i dalam cara-cara yang memiliki konsekuensi hukum yang signifikan. Klasifikasi Hanafi atas ungkapan-ungkapan, meliputi khass, amm, musytarak, majaz, sarih, kinayah, dan lain-lain, serta aturan-aturan yang mengatur penafsirannya menyediakan landasan linguistik bagi penalaran hukum Hanafi.

Diskusi tentang perintah-perintah dan implikasi default mereka sangat penting. Posisi Hanafi tentang apakah perintah-perintah dalam teks-teks ilahi secara default menunjukkan kewajiban (wujub) atau sesuatu yang kurang ketat berbeda secara halus dari posisi-posisi Syafi'i dan memiliki konsekuensi terhadap bagaimana perintah-perintah Quranic dan hadis yang spesifik ditafsirkan. Syarah al-Bukhari mengurai perbedaan-perbedaan ini dengan cermat dan membela posisi Hanafi terhadap kritik-kritik Syafi'i.

Pembahasan tentang Sunnah mencerminkan pendekatan khas mazhab Hanafi. Al-Bukhari menjelaskan dan membela posisi Hanafi tentang bobot hukum hadis ahad, kondisi-kondisi di mana hadis lemah dapat dikutip sebagai dalil hukum, dan hubungan antara Sunnah dan Al-Quran dalam pengkhususan dan pembatasan. Pendekatan Hanafi lebih permisif dalam mengutip hadis lemah dan lebih ketat dalam mengizinkan Sunnah membatasi keumuman Al-Quran, posisi-posisi yang memerlukan pembenaran cermat terhadap keberatan-keberatan Syafi'i.

Bagian-bagian tentang istihsan mendapat pembahasan yang ekstensif, sebagaimana dapat diharapkan mengingat bahwa metodologi ini adalah kontribusi Hanafi yang paling kontroversial dalam usul fikih. Al-Bukhari menyajikan pembelaan sistematis atas istihsan, mengembangkan penjelasan al-Bazdawi tentang landasannya dan merespons kecaman terkenal asy-Syafi'i bahwa ia setara dengan kesewenang-wenangan. Pembelaan ini membedakan istihsan dari preferensi yang sewenang-wenang dan menunjukkan bagaimana kriterianya membatasi penerapannya.

Pembahasan tentang ijmak mencakup posisi-posisi khas mazhab Hanafi tentang jenis-jenis ijmak mana yang mengikat dan bagaimana ijmak ditetapkan. Keterbukaan khusus mazhab Hanafi terhadap ijmak penduduk Madinah sebagaimana diwakili dalam pendekatan Malik, dibandingkan dengan pembatasan Syafi'i atas ijmak yang mengikat pada kesepakatan ulama universal, dibahas dan dijelaskan.

Tema yang berulang sepanjang karya ini adalah hubungan antara posisi-posisi yang diriwayatkan dari para ulama Hanafi awal dan prinsip-prinsip teoritis yang menjelaskannya. Proyek al-Bukhari adalah sekaligus mengartikulasikan teori yang ketat dan menunjukkan bahwa teori ini dengan benar menjelaskan hukum-hukum dari generasi pendiri mazhab.
content_ar: The treatment of language theory in the Kashf al-Asrar is one of the most المهم parts of the work. Al-Bazdawi's original text, and البخاري's expansion of it, articulates the الحنفي theory of linguistic expression in considerable detail — a theory that differs from the الشافعي approach in ways that have المهم legal consequences. The الحنفي classification of expressions (khass, 'amm, mushatarak, majaz, sarih, kinayah, and others) and the rules governing their interpretation provide the linguistic foundation for الحنفي legal reasoning. The discussion of commands and their default implications is particularly المهم. The الحنفي position on whether commands in divine texts default to الالتزام (wujub) or something less stringent differs subtly from الشافعي positions and has consequences for how specific Qur'anic and الحديث directives are interpreted. البخاري's commentary unpacks these differences with care and defends the الحنفي position against الشافعي critiques. The treatment of the السنة reflects the الحنفي school's distinctive approach. البخاري explains and defends the الحنفي position on the legal weight of ahad (single-chain) الحديث, the conditions under which weak الحديث can be cited as legal evidence, and the relationship between السنة and القرآن الكريم in specification and restriction. The الحنفي approach is more permissive in citing weak الحديث and more restrictive in allowing the السنة to restrict Qur'anic generalities — positions that require careful justification against الشافعي objections. The sections on istihsan receive extensive treatment, as might be expected given that this methodology is the most controversial distinctively الحنفي contribution to الأصول al-الفقه. البخاري presents a systematic defense of istihsan, developing al-Bazdawi's account of its foundations and responding to Al-الشافعي's famous condemnation of it as equivalent to arbitrariness. The defense distinguishes istihsan from arbitrary preference and shows how its criteria constrain its application. The treatment of الإجماع' (consensus) includes the الحنفي school's distinctive positions on what types of consensus are binding and how consensus is established. The الحنفي school's particular openness to the consensus of the people of المدينة المنورة (as represented in مالك's approach) compared to the الشافعي restriction of binding consensus to universal العلمي agreement is discussed and explained. A recurring theme throughout the work is the relationship between the transmitted positions of the early الحنفي masters and the theoretical principles that explain them. البخاري's project is simultaneously to articulate a rigorous theory and to show that this theory correctly explains the rulings of the school's founding generation.
